description: run the VSS incident-notification webhook relay (fans incidents out to Slack / Dashboard backends)
rules: VLM real-time mode only — CV deployments do not run this relay
Slack backend requires SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (xoxb-...) with chat:write and SLACK_CHANNEL_ID
VST_ENDPOINT is required at boot — the server exits if it is unset (used to resolve video clip URLs)
starts a long-lived FastAPI server (default 0.0.0.0:9090) that posts live incidents to remote backends
never call rtvi-vlm directly; this relay only receives incident webhooks and fans them out
usage: Set SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_CHANNEL_ID, VST_ENDPOINT (NOTIFY_BACKENDS defaults to 'dashboard'; set 'slack' or 'slack,dashboard'). The porter writes a .env under RECORD_STORE, launches server.py, and records the launch as one JSON audit line. Output: notify.json.
limitation: Requires the matching VSS alerts profile deployed and reachable in VLM real-time mode. NGC-hosted models/NIMs are subject to rate-limits, GPU-memory needs, and license terms; concurrency/storage limits depend on host hardware and the profile compose file. The server needs pip packages (fastapi, uvicorn, slack-sdk, python-dotenv, httpx, websockets) and a valid Slack bot token / reachable VST endpoint.
